engineIntelligence.h2: Aston Martin DB9 Engines - Codes, Common Failures & Replacement Costs
engineIntelligence.description: The cards below cover the V12 engines fitted to the Aston Martin DB9 in the UK - including documented failure causes like ticking noise and coil pack issues, production years across Coupe and Volante variants, and typical rebuilt engine costs based on current UK market data.

cards.altText: Aston Martin DB9 AM11 engine - petrol 5.9L replacement UK
knownFailures: Ticking noise from hydraulic lash adjusters - documented across early Cosworth-built AM11 blocks, often requiring full rebuild.
Coil pack failure on cylinders 3, 9, 10 - common misfire pattern on pre-facelift units causing rough running.
Oval cylinder bore wear - specific to early Cosworth AM11 blocks, identified by specialist rebuilders as critical failure point.

cards.altText: Aston Martin DB9 AM11A engine - petrol 6.0L replacement UK
knownFailures: Lash adjuster ticking persists on facelift units - though improved, still documented in owner forums as common complaint.
Ignition coil degradation - similar cylinder-specific misfire patterns to pre-facelift, particularly on higher-mileage examples.
Oil consumption increase - reported on facelift AM11A units beyond 60k miles, often linked to valve stem seal wear.

cards.altText: Aston Martin DB9 Cosworth V12 engine - petrol 5.9L replacement UK specialist
knownFailures: Small-end bearing failure - documented on early Cosworth AM11 blocks, can lead to catastrophic bottom-end damage if ignored.
Cylinder liner ovalisation - unique to Cosworth-built blocks, requires specialist machining or full replacement.
Timing chain tensioner wear - less common but documented on high-mileage early units, can cause rattling on cold start.
cards.replacementCost: Typical UK rebuilt engine: £22,000-£27,000 (supply only) Estimated from comparable AM11 units - actual quotes may vary due to Cosworth-specific rebuild requirements.

commonProblems.h2: Aston Martin DB9 Engine Problems - What It Costs to Fix & When Replacement Makes Sense
commonProblems.h3: The Aston Martin DB9's 5.9L/6.0L V12 engines (AM11/AM11A) are characterful but known for specific failure patterns that drive replacement demand. Every rebuilt engine supplied through EngineMarket includes upgraded internal components designed to address the original failure point - plus a minimum 12‑month unlimited mileage warranty. Use the cost comparisons below to decide between repair and replacement.

problems.h4: DB9 Engine Ticking Noise - Why AM11/AM11A V12 Engines Fail Prematurely
problems.affectedModels: Aston Martin DB9 2dr Coupe 6.0 V12, Volante 6.0 V12 (2004-2012, AM11 & AM11A)
problems.rootCause: Hydraulic lash adjusters wear or fail, causing persistent ticking; on early Cosworth AM11 blocks, oval cylinder bore wear exacerbates the issue.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Remove valve covers, replace faulty hydraulic lash adjusters, reset timing
repairOptions.longevity: May resolve ticking if caught early; not viable if oval bore wear present

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Strip cylinder heads, replace adjusters, valves, seals; machine heads if needed
repairOptions.longevity: Addresses valve-train issues; does not fix bottom-end or bore problems

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Supply & fit of fully rebuilt V12 with upgraded components addressing original failure points
repairOptions.longevity: Comprehensive solution; includes 12-month warranty; recommended for high-mileage or bore-wear cases

problems.recommendation: On a DB9 worth £25,000-£45,000, if ticking is accompanied by low compression or oil consumption, full replacement is often more cost-effective than repeated top-end repairs. For early Cosworth AM11 blocks, specialist assessment for oval bore is essential before committing to any repair.
problems.cta: Compare rebuilt AM11/AM11A engine prices for your Aston Martin DB9

problems.group: DB9 V12 Coil Pack & Misfire Failure
problems.h4: DB9 V12 Coil Pack & Misfire Failure - Cylinders 3, 9, 10 Pattern
problems.affectedModels: Aston Martin DB9 all variants (2004-2012, AM11 & AM11A)
problems.rootCause: Ignition coils on cylinders 3, 9 and 10 are prone to heat-related degradation, causing misfires, rough running and EML illumination.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Replace faulty ignition coils on affected cylinders, clear fault codes
repairOptions.longevity: Effective if caught early; coils may fail again if underlying heat management issues persist

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Replace all 12 coils, spark plugs, leads; inspect wiring loom and ECU connections
repairOptions.longevity: More comprehensive; reduces risk of repeat failures; recommended for vehicles over 70k miles

repairOptions.tier: Full Replacement - Rebuilt Engine with Upgraded Ignition
repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Supply & fit of rebuilt V12 with upgraded ignition components and thermal management
repairOptions.longevity: Best for high-mileage units with multiple failures; includes warranty and preventative upgrades

problems.recommendation: If misfires are isolated to cylinders 3, 9, 10 and compression is good, coil replacement is cost-effective. However, if misfires persist after coil replacement or are accompanied by ticking, assess for broader engine health before investing further.
problems.cta: Get quotes for Aston Martin DB9 V12 misfire repair or replacement

problems.h4: DB9 Oval Cylinder Bore Failure - Cosworth AM11 Specific
problems.affectedModels: Aston Martin DB9 early production Coupe/Volante (2004-2006, AM11 Cosworth-built blocks)
problems.rootCause: Early Cosworth-assembled AM11 blocks can develop oval-shaped cylinder bores due to manufacturing tolerances, leading to excessive oil consumption, loss of compression and eventual failure.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: No preventative minor fix exists for oval bore wear
repairOptions.longevity: This failure mode requires block-level intervention

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Remove engine, machine block or fit cylinder liners, rebuild with new pistons/rings
repairOptions.longevity: Specialist-only procedure; viable only if block is otherwise sound; not all workshops can perform

repairOptions.tier: Full Replacement - Rebuilt Engine with Corrected Block
repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Supply & fit of rebuilt V12 using corrected block or upgraded replacement assembly
repairOptions.longevity: Recommended solution for confirmed oval bore; includes warranty and addresses root cause

problems.recommendation: If oval bore is confirmed via compression test or borescope inspection, replacement with a corrected rebuilt unit is almost always more reliable and cost-effective than attempting in-situ machining. Early Cosworth blocks require specialist assessment.
problems.cta: Compare Cosworth AM11 rebuilt engine prices for your Aston Martin DB9

problems.h4: DB9 Small-End Bearing Failure - Bottom-End Catastrophic Risk
problems.affectedModels: Aston Martin DB9 all variants (2004-2012, AM11 & AM11A, higher risk on early Cosworth blocks)
problems.rootCause: Connecting rod small-end bearings can wear prematurely under high load or poor lubrication, leading to knocking, loss of oil pressure and potential catastrophic engine seizure.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: No minor repair viable once bearing wear causes knocking
repairOptions.longevity: This failure is catastrophic; prevention via oil changes is key

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Remove engine, replace bearings, inspect crankshaft, rebuild bottom end
repairOptions.longevity: Viable only if crankshaft is undamaged; requires specialist assessment; risk of hidden damage

repairOptions.tier: Full Replacement - Rebuilt Engine with Upgraded Bearings
repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Supply & fit of fully rebuilt V12 with upgraded bearing specifications and oil system improvements
repairOptions.longevity: Recommended for confirmed bearing failure; includes warranty and preventative upgrades

problems.recommendation: If knocking is heard from the bottom end, stop driving immediately. Bearing failure can destroy the crankshaft within minutes. Replacement with a rebuilt unit featuring upgraded bearings is the safest long-term solution.
problems.cta: Get urgent quotes for Aston Martin DB9 V12 bearing failure replacement

finalCta.h4: Don't Let Engine Failure Write Off Your Aston Martin DB9
finalCta.paragraph: The DB9's V12 is a masterpiece of engineering, but known failure patterns like ticking noise, coil pack misfires, oval bore wear and bearing issues can lead to costly repairs. EngineMarket makes it simple to compare prices from trusted UK Aston Martin DB9 specialists who understand these specific failure modes. Every rebuilt engine includes upgraded components addressing the original weak points, plus a minimum 12‑month unlimited mileage warranty and nationwide delivery. Compare quotes today and get back on the road without the main dealer premium.
finalCta.buttonText: Compare Aston Martin DB9 Engine Replacement Prices Now →
finalCta.disclaimer: All prices are indicative UK market ranges based on historical quote data and repair requests from our network of engine specialists. Dealer pricing is estimated based on published main dealer labour rates and genuine OEM parts pricing. Specialist pricing reflects actual quote data from EngineMarket's UK network. Actual costs vary depending on damage severity, vehicle condition, variant, and workshop location within the UK. Use our free comparison tool to get tailored quotes for your specific Aston Martin DB9 and engine code. Every engine supplied through EngineMarket carries a minimum 12‑month unlimited mileage warranty - always confirm exact terms with your chosen supplier.

engineSizes.h2: Aston Martin DB9 Engines by Size - Find the Right Displacement for Your Variant
engineSizes.intro: Every displacement fitted to the Aston Martin DB9 is listed below, with compatible engine codes, production years and common failure notes to help you identify the correct replacement.

groups.title: Aston Martin DB9 Petrol Engine Sizes

items.title: Aston Martin DB9 5.9L Petrol Engines
items.description: The 5.9L (5,935cc) naturally aspirated V12 powered all pre-facelift DB9 models from 2004-2008, delivering 470bhp with Cosworth engineering input. This displacement is associated with the AM11 engine code and represents the majority of early DB9 replacement demand, particularly for ticking noise and oval bore issues.
commonFailurePoints: oval cylinder bore wear on early Cosworth blocks
items.cta: Compare Aston Martin DB9 5.9L petrol engine prices

items.title: Aston Martin DB9 6.0L Petrol Engines
items.description: The revised 6.0L (5,935cc rounded) V12 with AM11A code powered facelift DB9 models from 2008-2012, featuring revised internals and increased output to 510bhp. While improved over the pre-facelift unit, lash adjuster ticking and coil pack issues persist, making this displacement a common replacement target for later-model owners.
commonFailurePoints: Lash adjuster ticking (improved but still present)
items.cta: Compare Aston Martin DB9 6.0L petrol engine prices

faq.h2: Aston Martin DB9 Engine Replacement - Frequently Asked Questions

items.question: Is an Aston Martin DB9 engine expensive to replace?
items.answer: Yes, DB9 V12 replacement typically costs £17,000-£27,000 for a rebuilt unit (supply only), reflecting the complex Cosworth-engineered V12 and specialist rebuild requirements.
keyPoints: AM11/AM11A V12 rebuilds require specialist machining and upgraded components
Labour costs are high due to complex V12 removal and installation procedures
Warranty coverage (12-month minimum) adds value versus private used engine purchases
items.cta: Compare rebuilt AM11/AM11A engine prices for your Aston Martin DB9

items.question: What causes the DB9 engine ticking noise?
items.answer: Ticking is primarily caused by hydraulic lash adjuster wear on AM11/AM11A V12 engines, with early Cosworth blocks also susceptible to oval cylinder bore wear exacerbating the issue.
keyPoints: Lash adjusters can fail between 40,000-80,000 miles on DB9 V12 engines
Oval bore wear on early Cosworth AM11 blocks requires specialist assessment
Ignition coil failure on cylinders 3, 9, 10 can mimic or accompany ticking symptoms
items.cta: Get quotes for Aston Martin DB9 ticking noise repair or replacement

items.question: Should I rebuild or replace my DB9 V12 engine?
items.answer: For DB9s with confirmed oval bore wear, bearing knock or high mileage (>70k), full replacement with a rebuilt unit is typically more cost-effective than repeated repairs.
keyPoints: Rebuild addresses specific failures; replacement provides comprehensive warranty coverage
Cosworth AM11 blocks require specialist machining not all workshops can perform
Factor in vehicle value (£25k-£45k) when deciding between repair investment and replacement
items.cta: Compare DB9 engine rebuild versus replacement costs from UK specialists

items.question: Which DB9 year has the most engine problems?
items.answer: 2004-2006 pre-facelift DB9 models with early Cosworth AM11 blocks show highest failure rates for oval bore wear and lash adjuster ticking.
keyPoints: Early production Cosworth AM11 blocks have documented oval cylinder bore issues
2005 model year appears most frequently in owner forum failure discussions
Facelift AM11A engines (2008+) show improved reliability but retain known failure patterns
items.cta: Check year-specific Aston Martin DB9 engine replacement prices

items.question: Can I replace a DB9 V12 engine myself?
items.answer: No, DB9 V12 replacement requires specialist tools, ECU coding expertise and Aston Martin-specific knowledge; professional installation is strongly recommended.
keyPoints: V12 removal/installation requires engine crane, transmission support and specialist fixtures
ECU adaptation and calibration post-installation needs dealer-level diagnostic equipment
Incorrect installation can cause catastrophic damage to new/rebuilt engine
items.cta: Find Aston Martin DB9 engine supply & fit specialists in your area

items.question: Where can I find a DB9 engine with warranty?
items.answer: EngineMarket's network of vetted UK specialists supplies rebuilt DB9 V12 engines with minimum 12-month unlimited mileage warranty as standard.
keyPoints: Warranty terms vary between suppliers; always confirm coverage details before purchase
Rebuilt engines with upgraded components addressing known failures offer best warranty value
Avoid private sales without verifiable compression test results and service history
items.cta: Compare warrantied Aston Martin DB9 replacement engine options

items.question: How long does a DB9 engine replacement take?
items.answer: Professional DB9 V12 replacement typically takes 5-10 working days depending on engine availability, workshop schedule and post-installation calibration requirements.
keyPoints: Engine supply lead times vary; rebuilt units may require 2-4 weeks preparation
Installation itself takes 2-3 days for experienced Aston Martin specialists
Post-installation ECU adaptation and road testing add 1-2 days to completion
items.cta: Get timeline estimates with your Aston Martin DB9 engine replacement quote

items.question: What is the difference between AM11 and AM11A engines?
items.answer: AM11 (2004-2008) is the pre-facelift 470bhp 5.9L V12; AM11A (2008-2012) is the facelift 510bhp 6.0L V12 with revised internals and improved but not eliminated failure patterns.
keyPoints: AM11 associated with early Cosworth blocks and oval bore concerns
AM11A features revised lash adjusters and increased power output to 510bhp
Both codes share coil pack failure pattern on cylinders 3, 9, 10
items.cta: Compare AM11 versus AM11A Aston Martin DB9 engine prices

items.question: Do I need specialist fitting for DB9 engine?
items.answer: Yes, DB9 V12 installation requires Aston Martin-specialist expertise for correct ECU coding, calibration and torque procedures to avoid damaging the new/rebuilt engine.
keyPoints: Specialist garages have DB9-specific tools, fixtures and diagnostic equipment
ECU adaptation post-installation requires dealer-level software access
Incorrect torque procedures on V12 can cause head gasket or bearing failure
items.cta: Find vetted Aston Martin DB9 engine fitting specialists near you

items.question: Can I upgrade my DB9 engine for more power?
items.answer: While aftermarket supercharger kits exist for the DB9 V12, engine replacement should prioritise reliability upgrades addressing known failures before considering performance modifications.
keyPoints: Upgraded lash adjusters and bearings should be specified on any rebuilt AM11/AM11A
Supercharger installations require supporting modifications to cooling and fuel systems
Warranty coverage may be voided by non-OEM performance modifications
items.cta: Discuss reliability-focused rebuild options for your Aston Martin DB9 V12
